signals.headline: IMF Article IV Consultation (April 2, 2026): Minimal 2026 Rate Cuts Projected | NO aggressive easing | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
IMF Article IV Consultation (April 2, 2026): Minimal 2026 Rate Cuts Projected | NO aggressive easing | HIGH confidence** [1]

The IMF forecasts U.S. GDP at 2.4% for 2026 (up from 2.0% in 2025), core PCE inflation at 2.6% by year-end (down from 2.9%), and fed funds rate easing only from 3.6% to 3.4%, citing limited room for cuts absent labor market deterioration.[1] Tariffs (effective rate 7-8.5%)
